title: Started Learning Coding
subtitle: Beginning my journey into web development
description: Began learning HTML, CSS, and JavaScript fundamentals.
overview: This marked the beginning of my programming journey. I started from zero knowledge and focused on understanding how websites are built and how frontend technologies work together.
story: Initially, web development felt overwhelming because everything was new. I spent time learning HTML structure, CSS styling, responsive layouts, and JavaScript basics. I practiced by recreating simple webpages and experimenting with browser developer tools.
problem: No prior technical knowledge and difficulty understanding coding concepts.
goal: Learn web development fundamentals and become capable of building simple websites independently.
outcome: Built confidence with frontend basics and developed a strong learning habit.
reflection: This phase taught me discipline and helped me understand that programming becomes easier through repeated practice.

title: Built First Project
subtitle: Created and deployed my first portfolio website
description: Created and deployed my first portfolio website.
overview: After learning fundamentals, I wanted to build something practical and publicly visible.
story: I designed and developed my first portfolio website to showcase projects and skills. This helped me understand component structure, UI organization, deployment, and responsive layouts in a real-world context.
problem: Needed practical experience applying concepts learned during self-study.
goal: Build a real project and deploy it online.
outcome: Successfully launched a portfolio website and gained confidence building complete projects.
reflection: Building a real project helped me connect theory with practical implementation.

title: Got Internship
subtitle: Joined a software company as Frontend Developer Intern
description: Joined a software company as a Frontend Developer Intern.
overview: This was my transition from self-learning into professional software development.
story: Working in a company environment introduced me to collaboration, deadlines, real-world debugging, and production-level frontend development.
goal: Learn professional workflows and contribute to real products.
outcome: Gained hands-on experience in frontend engineering and collaboration.
reflection: This experience helped me understand what software engineering looks like in practice.

title: Promoted to Full-Time
subtitle: Transitioned from intern to full-time developer
description: Successfully transitioned from intern to full-time developer.
overview: Receiving a full-time role validated the skills and trust developed during internship.
story: I started taking ownership of tasks, improving system understanding, and contributing to more critical features.
problem: Needed to transition from learner mindset to ownership mindset.
outcome: Earned a permanent position and larger responsibilities.
reflection: This phase helped me become more confident in my engineering decisions.

title: Started New Job
subtitle: Joined a company building enterprise applications
description: Joined a new company to work on enterprise applications.
overview: A new chapter focused on larger systems and scalable applications.
story: The role exposed me to enterprise-level systems, collaboration across teams, and performance-focused engineering.
problem: Needed to adapt to a new environment and larger architecture.
goal: Grow into a stronger engineer working on scalable systems.
outcome: Currently learning and contributing to enterprise software.
reflection: This role continues to push my technical growth.
